title: Health & Wellness Seminar - Digital Health Offerings at Northeast Georgia Health System
description: Northeast Georgia Health System’s digital health offerings provide patients with a seamless, connected care experience through tools such as virtual visits, remote patient monitoring, online scheduling, secure messaging, and expanded virtual‑first primary care options. These services are designed to increase convenience, improve access to timely care, and support patients in managing their health from anywhere. Presented by: Melissa Forster-Green Melissa Forster‑Green serves as the Director of Digital Health for Northeast Georgia Health System, bringing more than 20 years of healthcare leadership experience. She leads the development and execution of the organization’s digital health strategy with a strong focus on elevating the patient experience across the digital journey, improving access to care, and strengthening patient–clinician connectivity. Melissa partners with leaders, clinicians, and frontline teams to evaluate, implement, and optimize technologies that shape NGHS’s digital front door. Her combined clinical nursing background and strategic expertise enable her to drive meaningful, system‑level impact. 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: Small Business Seminar - Stop Being the Bottleneck: How to Build a Business That Outlasts You
description: Most business owners are building something that depends entirely on them, and they don’t realize it until it’s too late. In this talk, Tess Helmandollar breaks down the 10 Pillars of Transferable Value from her book The Legacy Link , revealing what actually makes a business scalable, sustainable, and sellable. Drawing from experience and hard-earned perspective, she’ll challenge the way you think about growth, leadership, and control, and introduce new concepts from her upcoming work, Toxic Grace , that exposes the hidden habits quietly holding businesses back. Whether you plan to sell, scale, or simply breathe again, this session will show you how to build a business that works for you, and outlasts you. Tess Helmandollar is the founder of The Cognitive Creative and Growth Pro Partners, where she helps service-based and trade businesses turn chaos into scalable, sellable companies. With a background in journalism and business operations, she’s known for cutting through noise, identifying what’s holding a business back, and building the systems that unlock real growth. Inspired by her father’s HVAC business—and what was lost when it couldn’t be transitioned, Tess authored The Lega

title: Small Business Seminar - Applied AI for Business: Understanding the Superpowers that AI can Unlock for your Business
description: About the presentation: AI is a superpower. Everyone knows that it’s powerful, but few people really understand how to wield it. This presentation will help you to break through the first wall in your understanding so that you can grasp the simple and easy, and consider the complex and profound implications for AI on your business today. About the speaker: Ben Nichol is an active Forward Deployed Engineer . His work is centered around embedding with businesses, understanding their needs, and helping them to understand and deploy AI powered solutions. Before working in AI, Ben spent over 15 years in the games industry working for organizations like Red Bull, the New York Mets, and Mr. Beast. 8:15 - 8:30 AM Networking 8:30 - 9:30 AM Seminar 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: Finance & Administrative Forum
description: Finance & Administrative Forum Reducing Tax Liability & Improving Cash Flow Through Tax Credits Whether you're actively growing, training your team, or simply want to know what you might be leaving on the table, this session will show you where to look and how to claim those credits. Tuesday, August 11 11:45 AM - 1:00 PM Carroll Daniel 330 Main Street SW All Businesses look for ways to reduce their tax liability and improve cash flow. Tax credits are one of the most direct ways to do this; however, most businesses don't know what they qualify for, or are not aware credits exist. Josh Hollum with TaxCredible, the largest submitter to the State of Georgia for the Retaining Tax Credit, breaks down what's available and why so much goes unclaimed. Drawing on work helping businesses reduce tax liability and free up cash flow through state and federal credits, Josh will discuss what qualifies, what doesn't, and how the dollars you recover can help you fund your next hire, invest in your business, or simply let you breath easier at tax time. 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: Business After Hours - Love Your Story
description: Business After Hours Love Your Story Thursday, August 13, 2026 5:00 - 7:00 PM 4436 Thompson Bridge Rd Gainesville, GA 30506 REGISTER HERE Love Your Story invites you to an evening of connection, community, and a first-hand look at the future of belonging in Hall County. As a Gainesville-based nonprofit, Love Your Story creates spaces of belonging for families impacted by disability and medical complexity through free temporary housing, caregiver support programs, and inclusive community initiatives. Since 2022, Love Your Story has served families from across the country who travel to Northeast Georgia for their child’s therapy and medical care. Join us at 4436 Thompson Bridge Road in Gainesville, the future home of the Dream With Us Campus. Guests will have the opportunity to walk the newly purchased 8-acre property, connect with local leaders and community members, and hear the vision for what is being built. Throughout the evening, attendees can expect networking, light refreshments, music, giveaways, and an inside look at the four-phase master plan that will one day include universal design homes, a host home, and a gathering space designed specifically for families navigating d

title: South Hall Business Coalition - Michelin Raceway Road Atlanta
description: Michelin Raceway Road Atlanta Thursday, September 3, 2026 9:00 - 10:00 AM 5300 Winder Hwy Braselton, GA 30517 REGISTER HERE Meet us at the Michelin Tower, and get all the updates on the exciting 29th Annual Motul Petit Le Mans (September 30 - October 3). For the past 25+ years, Motul Petit Le Mans has been THE sporting event in the southeast to experience. With four days of on-track battles, culminating in the 10-hour IMSA WeatherTech Championship finale showdown on Saturday, October 3, the action is nonstop. Enjoy the Kids Zone, vendors, food trucks, displays, car corrals, camping, and so much more, this event has it all, and at family-friendly prices, too! For event details and tickets, visit MichelinRacewayRoadAtlanta.com $8.00 Member $15.00 Non-Member Light breakfast included 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: Small Business Seminar - Expanding Small Business Capabilities through Higher Education Partnerships
description: About the presentation: Small businesses may fail to scale due to lack of access to resources. Often times, partnering with local technical colleges and universities is not included in discussions when exploring options to scale a business. However, technical colleges and universities are a good resource for small businesses seeking talent, development, and funding support. This session will highlight various ways that small businesses can expand capabilities by partnering with higher education institutions to build a talent pipeline, provide upskilling opportunities for current employees, and to pursue research and grant funding. About the speaker: Dr. Shatrela Washington-Hubbard serves as the Swinton A. Griffith III Dean of the Jacobs College of Business & Media at Brenau University. She holds an earned doctor of philosophy in learning, leadership, and organization development from the University of Georgia. Her professional experience includes industry experience in the fields of human resources and education. Dr. Washington-Hubbard’s research agenda focuses on topics in entrepreneurship, experiential learning, and career development. 8:15 - 8:30 AM Networking 8:30 - 9:30 AM Sem
